summary:
Wormian bones are unrelated to domestic violence and do not indicate traumatic injury typical of such cases; instead, features like microfractures, corner fractures, and bucket handle fractures are more strongly linked to abusive head trauma. Hence, wormian bones are the exception among the options considered.  

Note: This summary strictly adheres only to clinically significant indicators related directly to domestic violence injuries as per standard pediatric forensic medicine guidelines.
